pipeline.title: 用 I'm going to / I will 表达计划
pipeline.chunk: I'm going to / I will / I plan to

items.answer: I'm going to travel next month.
I plan to visit my family.

items.zh: 总有一天我会环游世界。
items.answer: Someday I will travel the world.
My goal is to speak English well.
I promise I will help you.
I hope to visit Paris one day.
By then, I will be ready.
In the future, I want to be a doctor.
I will eventually learn to drive.
My resolution is to exercise more.

pipeline.title: I'm going to [verb] [time] 计划表达操练

items.answer: I'm going to travel next month.
I'm going to visit my family next week.
I'm going to learn to cook tomorrow.
I'm going to sleep early tonight.
I'm going to buy a car eventually.

pipeline.title: plan / goal / resolution 一词多句

items.answer: I plan to travel next month.
I plan to learn a new skill.
What do you plan to do?
My goal is to speak English well.

items.zh: 她的目标是成为一名医生。
items.answer: Her goal is to become a doctor.
My resolution is to exercise more.
My New Year resolution is to save money.
Do you have a New Year resolution?

pipeline.title: promise / hope / expect 一词多句

items.answer: I promise I will help you.
I promise I will be on time.
I hope to visit Paris one day.

items.zh: 我希望今年能学会游泳。
items.answer: I hope to learn to swim this year.
What do you hope to do?
I expect to finish next week.
When do you expect to arrive?

pipeline.title: soon / someday / eventually 一词多句

items.answer: I hope to see you soon.

items.zh: 总有一天我会环游世界。
items.answer: Someday I will travel the world.
I will eventually learn to drive.

pipeline.title: 从计划到承诺逐步扩展

levels.label: 说计划
levels.en: I'm going to travel next month.

levels.label: 加目标
levels.en: I'm going to travel next month. My goal is to visit three countries.
levels.zh: 我下个月打算去旅行。我的目标是去三个国家。
levels.highlight: My goal is to visit three countries.

levels.label: 加承诺
levels.en: I'm going to travel next month. My goal is to visit three countries. I promise I will send you postcards.
levels.zh: 我下个月打算去旅行。目标是去三个国家。我保证给你寄明信片。
levels.highlight: I promise I will send you postcards.

pipeline.title: 用 will / probably / might 做预测

items.answer: It will probably be sunny tomorrow.
It is likely to snow tonight.
The forecast says it will rain.
There will be a storm tomorrow.
It will be sunny this weekend.
I wish it would stop raining.
The climate here is very warm.

pipeline.title: It will [verb] / It will be [adj] 预测操练

items.answer: There will be a storm this weekend.

pipeline.title: sunny / cloudy / storm / forecast 一词多句

items.answer: There will be a storm tomorrow.
There will be showers in the afternoon.
The forecast says it will rain.
What does the weather forecast say?
The forecast says it will be sunny tomorrow.

pipeline.title: probably / likely / perhaps 一词多句

items.answer: It will probably be sunny tomorrow.
It is likely to snow tonight.

pipeline.title: wish / hope 一词多句

items.answer: I wish it would stop raining.
I hope it will be sunny tomorrow.

pipeline.title: 从天气预测到行动建议逐步扩展

levels.label: 加建议
levels.en: It will probably rain tomorrow. You should take an umbrella.
levels.zh: 明天大概会下雨。你应该带把伞。

levels.label: 加替代
levels.en: It will probably rain tomorrow. You should take an umbrella. Perhaps we should stay home instead.
levels.zh: 明天大概会下雨。你应该带把伞。也许我们应该待在家。
levels.highlight: Perhaps we should stay home instead.

pipeline.title: 用 can / can't 表达能力和不能
pipeline.chunk: I can / I can't / Can you

items.zh: 我够不到最上面的架子。
items.answer: I can't reach the top shelf.
She has a talent for music.

items.zh: 说英语是个有用的技能。
items.answer: Speaking English is a useful skill.

pipeline.title: Can you [verb]? — Yes/No, I can/can't 能力问答操练

items.answer: Can you drive? — Yes, I can.
Can you swim? — No, I can't.
Can you help me? — Sure, I can.
Can you fix this? — I can try.
Can you handle this? — I can manage.

pipeline.title: skill / talent / manage 一词多句

items.zh: 说英语是个有用的技能。
items.answer: Speaking English is a useful skill.

items.zh: 驾驶是一项重要的技能。

items.answer: She has a talent for music.
He has a talent for drawing.
Can you manage on your own?

pipeline.title: fix / solve / handle 一词多句
reach / lift / ride 一词多句

items.zh: 我够不到最上面的架子。
items.answer: I can't reach the top shelf.
Can you reach it for me?
It's too high. I can't reach it.
It's too heavy. I can't lift it.
Can you help me lift this?
I want to learn to ride a horse.

pipeline.title: 从能力到解决问题逐步扩展

levels.label: 加问题
levels.en: I can swim, but I can't fix my phone. Can you help me?
levels.zh: 我会游泳，但我不会修手机。你能帮我吗？
levels.highlight: but I can't fix my phone. Can you help me?

levels.label: 加解决
levels.en: I can swim, but I can't fix my phone. Can you help me? — Sure, I can handle it.
levels.zh: 我会游泳但不会修手机。你能帮我吗？——当然，我能处理。

pipeline.title: 用 should / must / have to 表达建议和义务
pipeline.chunk: You should / You must / You have to

items.answer: You must wear a seat belt.

items.zh: 你得走了——我的公交5点开。
items.answer: I have to go now — my bus leaves at 5.
It's my duty to help you.
Don't force me to do it.
You don't have to go — it's an option.
It's necessary to be on time.
You are required to wear a mask.

pipeline.title: You should/must [verb] because [reason] 建议义务操练

items.zh: 你应该休息，因为看起来很累。
items.answer: You should rest because you look tired.

items.zh: 你必须系安全带，因为这是法律。
items.answer: You must wear a seat belt because it's the law.

items.zh: 你得走了，因为公交快来了。
items.answer: You have to go because the bus is coming.

items.zh: 你不必担心，因为一切都在掌控中。
items.answer: You don't have to worry because everything is under control.

items.zh: 你应该试试，因为真的很好。
items.answer: You should try it because it's really good.

pipeline.title: advise / recommend / suggest 一词多句

items.answer: I advise you to leave early.
What do you advise me to do?
I recommend you read this book.

pipeline.title: rule / law / duty 一词多句

items.answer: It's my duty to help you.
It's my duty to protect my family.
You are required to wear a mask.
This requires a booking in advance.

pipeline.title: necessary / vital / urgent 一词多句
pipeline.vocabMeaning: 必要的/至关重要的/紧急的

items.answer: It's necessary to be on time.

pipeline.title: 从建议到强制逐步扩展

levels.label: 加强制
levels.en: You should rest more. Actually, you must rest — the doctor said so.
levels.zh: 你应该多休息。其实你必须休息——医生说的。
levels.highlight: Actually, you must rest — the doctor said so.

levels.label: 加后果
levels.en: You must rest because it's vital for your health. You don't want to get sick.
levels.zh: 你必须休息，因为对你的健康至关重要。你不想生病吧。
levels.highlight: You don't want to get sick.

pipeline.title: 用 mustn't / don't have to 区分禁止和不必

items.answer: You must not go over the speed limit.
You don't have to come — it's optional.
You are not allowed to smoke here.
You must obey the traffic rules.

items.zh: 违反规则你必须交罚款。
items.answer: You must pay a penalty if you break the rule.
Let me remind you of the rule.
I must warn you about the danger.

items.zh: 你不该冒不必要的风险。

items.answer: There is no exception to this rule.

items.zh: 使用塑料袋是被禁止的。
items.answer: There is a ban on plastic bags.

pipeline.title: You mustn't [verb] because [reason] 禁止操练

items.zh: 你不能超速，因为有速度限制。
items.answer: You mustn't speed because there is a speed limit.

items.zh: 你不能在这里停车，因为这是禁止的。
items.answer: You mustn't park here because it's forbidden.

items.zh: 你不能抽烟，因为这是禁烟区。
items.answer: You mustn't smoke because this is a no-smoking area.

items.zh: 你不必来，因为这是可选的。
items.answer: You don't have to come because it's optional.

items.zh: 你不必付钱，因为我已经付了。
items.answer: You don't have to pay because I already paid.

pipeline.title: forbid / prohibit / ban 一词多句

items.answer: There is a ban on plastic bags.
There is a ban on smoking in public places.

pipeline.title: strict / limit / penalty 一词多句

items.answer: You must not go over the speed limit.
There is a penalty for breaking the rules.

pipeline.title: remind / warn / risk 一词多句

items.answer: Let me remind you of the rule.
Let me remind you to take an umbrella.
I must warn you about the danger.

items.zh: 你不该冒不必要的风险。

items.answer: Are you willing to take the risk?

pipeline.title: 从规则提醒到后果逐步扩展

levels.label: 加原因
levels.en: You must not smoke here because smoking is prohibited in this area.
levels.zh: 你不能在这里抽烟，因为这个区域禁止吸烟。
levels.highlight: because smoking is prohibited in this area.

levels.label: 加后果
levels.en: You must not smoke here because smoking is prohibited. If you do, you must pay a penalty.
levels.zh: 你不能在这抽烟因为禁止吸烟。如果抽了，你得交罚款。
levels.highlight: If you do, you must pay a penalty.

pipeline.title: 用 may / might / don't have to / mustn't 表达可能和不必
pipeline.chunk: may / might / don't have to / mustn't

items.answer: You don't have to come — it's optional.
I suppose you may be right.
There is a chance it might rain.

items.zh: 这个任务可选——你不必做。
items.answer: This task is optional — you don't have to do it.
You are not permitted to enter.
I may not dare to try it.

pipeline.title: It may/might [verb] — but you mustn't [verb] 对比操练

items.zh: 可能会下雨，但你不必担心。
items.answer: It may rain, but you don't have to worry.

items.zh: 他可能会迟到，但你不应该生气。
items.answer: He might be late, but you shouldn't get angry.

items.zh: 情况可能会变，但你不能放弃。
items.answer: Things may change, but you mustn't give up.

items.zh: 可能会很难，但你不必一个人面对。
items.answer: It might be hard, but you don't have to face it alone.

items.zh: 也许我是错的，但你不可以这样说话。
items.answer: I might be wrong, but you mustn't speak like that.

pipeline.title: chance / guess / suppose 一词多句

items.answer: There is a chance it might rain.
Is there a chance of success?
There is no chance at all.
I guess it might be too late.
I suppose you may be right.

pipeline.title: optional / unnecessary / compulsory 一词多句
pipeline.vocabMeaning: 可选的/不必要的/强制的

items.zh: 这个任务可选——你不必做。
items.answer: This task is optional — you don't have to do it.

items.zh: 这是可选的还是必须的？

items.answer: It's unnecessary to worry so much.

items.zh: 所有学生必须参加——这是强制的。
items.answer: All students must attend — it's compulsory.

pipeline.title: permit / refuse / hesitate 一词多句

items.answer: You are not permitted to enter.

items.zh: 如果有问题，别犹豫联系我。
items.answer: Don't hesitate to contact me if you have questions.

pipeline.title: 从可能到不必逐步扩展

levels.label: 加不必
levels.en: It may rain tomorrow, but you don't have to bring an umbrella — I have one.
levels.zh: 明天可能下雨，但你不必带伞——我有一把。
levels.highlight: but you don't have to bring an umbrella — I have one.

levels.label: 加可选
levels.en: It may rain tomorrow, but you don't have to bring an umbrella. It's optional — I have extras.
levels.zh: 明天可能下雨但你不必带伞。这是可选的——我有多余的。
